summaryText: Pastor Todd opened the sermon by sharing a personal story about driving in the Swiss Alps, using it as a metaphor for navigating life's fears. He then introduced the story of Gideon from Judges 6-7, who was hiding in fear after Israel had been impoverished and oppressed by the Midianites. The central message was that God meets us in our 'cave' experiences—our places of fear, loss, and hiding—and provides a way out. Pastor Todd outlined four principles for overcoming fear: first, we get stronger and smarter through prayer, which is a simple conversation with a God who loves us. Second, we must use God's Word as our guide, trusting His promises over our circumstances. Third, we can be courageous because God is with us, in us, and for us, even when He removes people or resources we think we need. Finally, when it's time, we must pick up the pace and go all-in, trusting God to fight our battles. The sermon concluded with the powerful image of Gideon's 300 men, who won a victory not by their own strength but by obeying God's unconventional strategy, demonstrating that our greatest weapon against fear is a 'yes' to God.
mainPoints: We get stronger and smarter through prayer, which is a conversation with a God who loves us and longs to be close.
We must be courageous because God is with us, in us, and for us, even when He pares down our resources to ensure we rely on Him.
When it's time to face our fears, we must pick up the pace, go all-in, and trust God to fight the battles we cannot win on our own.

versesMentioned.text: The LORD is my light and my salvation—whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life—of whom shall I be afraid?
The Midianites so impoverished the Israelites that they cried out to the LORD for help.
For I desire mercy, not sacrifice, and acknowledgment of God rather than burnt offerings.
The LORD confides in those who fear him; he makes his covenant known to them.
Come near to God and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded.

The LORD turned to him and said, 'Go in the strength you have and save Israel out of Midian’s hand. Am I not sending you?' ... The LORD answered, 'I will be with you, and you will strike down all the Midianites, leaving none alive.'
Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the LORD your God will be with you wherever you go.
Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.

Monday.scripture: Psalms 27:1 (NIV) - The LORD is my light and my salvation—whom shall I fear?
Monday.focus: Identify one area of fear or a 'cave' you are hiding in.
Monday.action: Be honest with God about that fear in a simple, sincere prayer.
Monday.prayer: Lord, you are my light. I bring my fear of __________ to you today. Help me to trust you as my stronghold.

Tuesday.scripture: James 4:8 (NIV) - Come near to God and he will come near to you.
Tuesday.focus: Developing closeness with God through conversation.
Tuesday.action: Throughout your day, 'talk' to God about small decisions and thoughts as you would a friend.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I draw near to you today. Help me to remember you are always with me and desire to hear from me.

Wednesday.scripture: Joshua 1:9 (NIV) - Be strong and courageous... for the LORD your God will be with you.
Wednesday.action: Write down this verse. When a fearful thought arises, speak this truth over yourself.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, your Word says you are with me. I choose courage today, not because I am strong, but because you are with me.

Thursday.scripture: Judges 6:14 (NIV) - Go in the strength you have... Am I not sending you?
Thursday.focus: Using what you have, not waiting for what you've lost.
Thursday.action: Take one practical step forward in an area where you feel inadequate, trusting that God is sending you.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, I feel weak, but you ask me to go with the strength I have. I say 'yes' to the next step you have for me.

Friday.scripture: Psalms 23:4 (NIV) - I will fear no evil, for you are with me.
Friday.focus: Preaching truth to yourself in the 'valley'.
Friday.action: Memorize this phrase: 'I will fear no evil.' Declare it out loud when you feel anxiety rising.
Friday.prayer: Shepherd of my soul, even in dark places, you are with me. Your presence is my comfort and my courage.

Saturday.scripture: Judges 7:15-22 (NIV) - Summary: Gideon obeyed, and God caused the enemy army to turn on itself.
Saturday.focus: Going all-in and watching God work.
Saturday.action: Reflect on a situation where you need to stop striving and simply obey God's direction. Commit to trusting Him for the outcome.
Saturday.prayer: God, sometimes the battle is yours to fight. I choose to obey, to stand firm, and to watch for your victory. I trust you completely.
